summary: Kenneth teaches a disciplined covered call strategy executed in ~1 hour/week: scout downtrending stocks on Sunday evening, evaluate premiums Monday morning at market open (9:30am), target high-beta volatile stocks for 2-7% weekly premiums, and aim for assignment as the primary goal. He emphasizes buying dips rather than overbought highs and accepting smaller but consistent gains over maximum returns.
takeaways: Buy stocks in downtrends (not at all-time highs or overbought levels) to increase probability of stock recovery and assignment
Target high-beta stocks that are more volatile than overall market to generate higher premiums (3-7% per contract)
Sunday evening: spend 30 minutes creating watchlist of 15-20 stocks in downtrends that could rebound
Monday at 9:30am market open: spend 10-15 minutes reviewing premiums for all positions and calculating per-contract gains
Adjust risk tolerance by comparing premium opportunities: accept 2-3% gains to avoid earnings risk, or take 7% if risk parameters allow
Sell covered calls at strike prices slightly above purchase price (e.g., buy at $25, sell $26 strike) accepting capped upside for consistent premium income
Goal is not maximum profit but consistent, achievable gains paired with high assignment probability

strategy_candidates.change: Establish a fixed weekly premium target ($1,250/mo ≈ $289/week) and systematically size positions across the 15-20 stock watchlist to hit that target consistently
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth mentions maintaining a 15-20 stock rotation and spending 10-15 minutes Monday morning calculating per-contract gains, suggesting a formulaic approach to position sizing that could be optimized to guarantee Brian's $1,250/month premium goal across the Premium Desk portfolio

title: I haven’t worked a real job since 2011
